extraction.one_line_summary: DÉMARE Arnaud won sprint in Cuneo after breakaway caught inside final kilometer, peloton miscalculating nearly lost 3-minute gap with 25km remaining.
tactical_patterns: TAGLIANI Filippo dropped on Colle di Nava while in breakaway, revealing insufficient power reserves for climbing breaks.
Peloton split with 30km remaining dropped 30 riders including PORTE Richie, showing UAE/Israel/Quickstep wind-driven echelons overpowered GC teams.
Breakaway doubled advantage to 7 minutes during Colle di Nava ascent, exposing sprinter teams' inability to control when climbers collaborate.
SINKELDAM Ramon led out with 600m remaining but faded badly, suggesting Groupama-FDJ overcommitted leadout man too early.

rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: abandoned while 4th GC after illness overnight, revealing vulnerability to heat/digestive issues in multi-week efforts
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: third stage win shows dominant sprint form, successfully navigated 150km heat and Colle di Nava without distress
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: neutral: third place suggests speed present but positioning/timing inferior to DÉMARE's late acceleration
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: caught in 30-rider split at 30km shows positioning weakness and declining GC ambitions
rider_signals.BAUHAUS Phil: positive: second place indicates genuine speed reserve, appears strongest DÉMARE challenger for remaining sprint stages
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: neutral: fourth place maintains consistency but lacks winning acceleration against top-tier sprinters
